label: OSINT Analyst
description: Conducts open source intelligence investigations — subject profiling, digital footprint analysis, geolocation, and intelligence product development for corporate, law enforcement, or government clients.

paths.name: Practitioner Track
paths.rationale: IntelTechniques OSIP is the practitioner gold standard — live 1-on-1 proctored scenario-based exam. PORP adds a cheaper accessible credential for portfolio validation. GSOA provides the GIAC brand credibility for enterprise and government hiring. Trace Labs CTF participation is mandatory portfolio-building alongside all three.
pros: OSIP is the most respected OSINT cert — unique live proctored scenario exam
GSOA is the first GIAC CyberLive hands-on OSINT cert — strong institutional credibility
PORP provides accessible entry validation before OSIP investment
Trace Labs CTF experience complements every cert in the stack
cons: Most US OSINT roles require security clearance — certs alone won't open government doors
OSIP requires ~$550 IntelTechniques training membership before exam eligibility (~$850 total)
GSOA is $9,800+ all-in with SEC587 training — employer funding recommended
Portfolio (Trace Labs, published investigations) matters as much as certs

prerequisites.experience: Basic internet research skills and familiarity with OSINT tools recommended. Trace Labs CTF participation before attempting OSIP strongly advised.
knowledge: Privacy and legal boundaries of OSINT

cert_sequence.notes: OSINTion Practical OSINT Research Professional — accessible entry credential at $399. Covers OSINT methodology, subject profiling, and investigation workflow. Good starting validation point before the OSIP investment. Participate in Trace Labs CTFs concurrently — they're the best portfolio-building activity available and directly prepare for OSIP's scenario format.

IntelTechniques OSINT Investigator Professional — developed by Michael Bazzell (Privacy, Security, and OSINT podcast). Unique live 1-on-1 proctored exam with Jason Edison using a scenario-based investigation format. $300 exam fee requires prior IntelTechniques training membership (~$550/year). Total ~$850. No renewal. The practitioner community considers OSIP the benchmark OSINT credential for methodology depth and exam rigor. Complete all IntelTechniques courses before attempting.

GIAC Open Source Intelligence Analyst — launched November 12, 2025, the first GIAC CyberLive hands-on OSINT cert. Associated with SANS SEC587. ~$9,800 all-in with training — employer funding strongly recommended. The GIAC brand adds government and enterprise hiring credibility that OSIP and PORP lack. With OSIP's methodology foundation established, GSOA's hands-on format validates the same skills in a proctored CyberLive environment.

paths.name: Budget Track
paths.rationale: For self-funded OSINT practitioners. PORP provides accessible entry, OSIP is the practitioner gold standard, and CTIA provides threat intelligence context that elevates OSINT work from passive research to actionable intelligence products. Total cost under $1,500.
pros: Under $1,500 total — the most affordable credentialed OSINT path
OSIP is the practitioner gold standard regardless of budget
CTIA adds threat intel context — makes OSINT work more operationally relevant
cons: No GIAC brand in the stack — limits government recognition
CTIA from EC-Council has mixed community reception
Trace Labs portfolio still essential alongside certs
OSIP total cost is ~$850 including mandatory training membership

cert_sequence.notes: OSINTion PORP — affordable entry credential. Validates OSINT methodology foundation before the OSIP investment. Run Trace Labs CTFs concurrently.
IntelTechniques OSIP — the practitioner gold standard. ~$850 total including mandatory training membership. Live 1-on-1 proctored scenario exam. Non-negotiable for serious OSINT practitioners.

EC-Council Certified Threat Intelligence Analyst — adds structured intelligence cycle methodology and threat actor profiling context to raw OSINT skills. MCQ-based, $499. Elevates OSINT work from passive research to intelligence product development.
